army_customisation.intro:
Army Customisation allows you to further individualise your force. Archetypes change how you build your army by moving around units in the Army Organisation Plan (AOP) or letting you take more of a unit than you would normally be allowed to. Legacies give access to armories and disciplines of legendary factions. Traits enhance your units with special abilities that they normally would not be able to have.

You may select up to the following:
0-1 Archetype, 0-1 Legacy, 0-2 Traits

If a Trait is taken, all models / units in the army must be upgraded with it, unless stated otherwise.

archetypes.name: 1st Company
archetypes.description:
The army may only consist of the following units:
- 1x Captain Dreadnought or 1x Lieutenant, 1x Chaplain or 1x Chaplain Dreadnought, 1x Emperor's Champion, 1x Librarian or 1x Librarian Dreadnought
- 1x Ancient, 1x Apothecary, Deathwing Knights, Dreadnoughts (including variants), Honor Guard, 1x Judiciar, 1x Company Champion, Terminators, 1x Techmarine
- Land Raider (including variants), Repulsor (including variants)
- Drop Pods
- HQ models must gain their "once per army" upgrade, if possible.
- Honor Guard and Terminators can be taken as Troops.
- Honor Guard units are not limited to 1x per HQ selection anymore.
- All units gain the special rule "Objective secured!".

archetypes.name: Death from Above
archetypes.description:
- Assault Squad units can be taken as Troops.
- Only Assault Squad units count towards the 25% Troops limitation.
- At least half of all Assault Squad models must start the game in reserves, even if the mission does not allow reserves.

archetypes.name: Drop Pod Assault
archetypes.description:
- All units must start the game as passengers inside a Drop Pod.
- Half of all Drop Pods in the army (round up) arrive automatically in the first battle round as reinforcements. The other half arrives automatically in the second battle round.

archetypes.name: Forlorn Brothers
archetypes.description:
The army may only consist of the following units:
- Creature units with the "Black Rage" equipment (see Blood Angels Armory).
- Dreadnoughts, Redemptor Dreadnoughts.
- Drop Pods, Impulsors, Razorbacks, Rhinos. 
- Death Company units can be taken as Troops and are not limited to one per army anymore.

archetypes.name: Legion
archetypes.description:
- The army has access to everything from the Horus Heresy Space Marines supplement.
- Only Troops from the Horus Heresy Space Marine Supplement count towards the 25% Troops limitation.

archetypes.name: Librarian Conclave
archetypes.description:
- The army gets 4 HQ slots in total and has only access to Librarians, Chief Librarians and Librarian Dreadnoughts as HQ models. Librarians gain the "Command Squad" ability.
- All of these models must be set up within 12" of each other. 
- While a Chief Librarian is within 12" of a Librarian, they can manifest all powers known to the Librarian and receive a +1 bonus to manifesting and dispelling psychic powers.

archetypes.name: Renegades
archetypes.description:
- Renegades are treated like "Chaos Space Marines" in the Ally matrix.
- May select one Trait from the "Chaos Space Marines" list instead of the normal selection.

archetypes.name: Swift as the Wind
archetypes.description:
- Bikes and Outriders can be taken as Troops.
- Outflanking units may be deployed on turn 1.
- All units with less than 12" Movement must start the game as passengers inside a transport.
- Units that have no transport option and less than 12" Movement cannot be taken at all.

legacies.name: Legacy of Aurelia
legacies.description: The army has access to the Blood Ravens Armory.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Alien Hunters
legacies.description:
The army has access to the Death Watch Armory, Inquisitors and Inquisition units. Inquisitors must select "Ordo Xenos" in the armory.
Each model can receive the "Special ammunition" equipment, regardless of whether it has access to the armory.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Angel
legacies.description: The army has access to the Blood Angels Armory and the Sanguine psychic discipline.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Crusader
legacies.description: The army has access to the Black Templars Armory and Prayers of the Faithful.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Damned
legacies.description: The army has access to the Relictors Armory.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Khan
legacies.description: The army has access to the White Scars Armory and the Stormspeaking psychic discipline.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Lion
legacies.description: The army has access to the Dark Angels Armory and the Interromancy psychic discipline.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Praetorian
legacies.description: The army has access to the Imperial Fists Armory and the Geokinesis psychic discipline.

legacies.name: Legacy of the Wolf
legacies.description: The army has access to the Space Wolves Armory and the Tempestus psychic discipline.

traits.name: Blitz Attack
traits.description: The unit can fire Assault weapons, Grenades and Pistols without a to hit penalty with Advance and Charge orders.

traits.name: Bolter Drill
traits.description: The unit gains the "Deflagrate(5+)" ability with Bolt weapons. Does not apply to "Heavy" weapons.

traits.name: Expanded Armory
traits.description: The army must select a second Legacy. Each model may only select items from one Legacy armory.

traits.name: Cursed Founding
traits.description:
Select one improvement that must be applied to any creature unit:
- Apparitions: The unit gains the "Deep Strike" ability and must use it during the DEPLOYMENT PHASE instead of being set up as normal. It can deep strike anywhere that isn't the enemy's deployment zone, but can scatter into it. Additionally, the unit gains the "Warded" ability.
- Aura of Doom: The unit gains the "Terrifying(-2)" ability.
- Berserkers: The unit gains the "Blind Rage" ability.
- Immolation: The model gains the "Retribution(1)" ability.
- Ossific Blades: The model gains the "Ossific Blades" weapon. (See Armory).

traits.name: Forged in Battle
traits.description: The unit can re-roll one armor penetration or wound roll per activation.

traits.name: Knowledge is Power
traits.description: The model can re-roll one psychic test per activation. Only for Psykers.

traits.name: Path of Damnation
traits.description: One model of the army must gain the "Demon weapon" equipment from the "Chaos Space Marines" codex to enhance one of its weapons (chosen from the Space Marine codex). The Demon weapon ability can be from any Armory.

traits.name: Purity above All
traits.description: One non-character model per squad with access to gear from the Armory can be upgraded to an Apothecary and gains the "Narthecium" ability.

traits.name: Red Thirst
traits.description: If the unit uses a Charge order or gets charged by an enemy unit, it gains +1 Strength until the end of the current battle round.

traits.name: Siege Experts
traits.description: The unit gains the "Sunder(1)" ability for all ranged attacks.

traits.name: Sons of Mars
traits.description: One non-character model per squad with access to gear from the Armory can be upgraded to a Techmarine and gains the "Blessing of the Omnissiah" ability.

traits.name: Stalwart
traits.description: The unit can reroll one armor save per battle round.

traits.name: Stoic
traits.description: The unit does not suffer the to hit penalty from Defensive Fire.

traits.name: Strike from the Shadows
traits.description: The unit gains the benefit of light cover until its first activation.

traits.name: The Flesh is Weak
traits.description: Creature models gain a 6+ invulnerability save. Vehicles repair either one Engine Damage or Weapon Damage on themselves during each Rally phase. Only for creature models that do not already have an invulnerability save from their datasheet or Armory.

traits.name: Unleashed Hunters
traits.description: If the unit uses a Charge order or gets charged by an enemy unit, it gains +1 to melee hit rolls until the end of the current battle round.
